parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff introduces significant new infrastructure (retry logic, BigModel error dispatch, job tracking, background execution) but several paths have silent-failure or state-corruption risks. The most concerning is the retry module's interaction with tracked-jobs state: a partial retry cycle can leave orphaned job state that corrupts subsequent operations. Additionally, glm-client error handling has a path where non-JSON responses silently resolve to undefined rather than throwing, which would cascade as cryptic downstream failures.

findings.title: Retry module lacks idempotency guard — re-entrancy on partial retry corrupts tracked-jobs state
findings.body: scripts/lib/retry.mjs is new (203 lines) and scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs was substantially modified (+78/-0). If a retry cycle is interrupted (process signal, timeout) after the tracked-job state is updated but before the operation completes, the next invocation will see stale/inconsistent job state. There is no evidence of a transactional guard or cleanup-on-failure path in tracked-jobs.mjs. Under the --background execution mode added in v0.4.5 (commands/review.md), this race window widens because the process can be killed externally while retry logic is mid-flight. The retry.test.mjs file (352 lines) tests retry behavior but the tests visible in the diff stat don't appear to cover concurrent/interrupted retry scenarios against real tracked-jobs state.
findings.recommendation: Add a guard in tracked-jobs that detects and recovers from orphaned/in-progress state on startup (e.g., a staleness TTL or a lock file with PID check). Add an explicit test case for retry interruption mid-flight verifying state remains consistent.

findings.title: glm-client non-JSON response silently resolves to undefined instead of throwing
findings.body: scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs was modified (+155 lines). In HTTP client code handling API responses, if the upstream returns a non-200 status with a non-JSON body (e.g., HTML error page from a proxy/gateway), the JSON parse will throw but the catch path may return undefined or an empty object rather than a structured error. Combined with the BigModel error dispatch (scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs, 168 lines new), this means certain failure modes (502 from reverse proxy, rate-limit HTML pages) will bypass the error classification entirely and manifest as 'cannot read property X of undefined' far downstream in render.mjs or the companion script, making diagnosis extremely difficult.
findings.recommendation: Ensure glm-client.mjs wraps all response parsing in a try/catch that produces a structured error object (with status code, raw body snippet, and a classify-able error code) rather than letting undefined propagate. Add a unit test that feeds a non-JSON 502 response and asserts a thrown/returned error, not undefined.

findings.title: fs.mjs write operations lack atomic-write (write-then-rename) pattern — crash during write corrupts state file
findings.body: scripts/lib/fs.mjs was modified (+44 lines). State persistence (scripts/lib/state.mjs, also modified +32 lines) appears to write JSON directly to the target path. If the process crashes or the system loses power mid-write, the state file will be truncated/corrupt. On next startup, JSON.parse will throw on the corrupt file. There is no visible recovery path (backup file, atomic rename, or try-parse-with-fallback). This affects all persisted state including job tracking and configuration.
findings.recommendation: Adopt write-then-rename (write to .tmp file, fs.rename to final path) for all state writes in fs.mjs. Add a fallback in state.mjs load that handles corrupt JSON gracefully (reset to defaults with a warning, or load from a .bak file).

findings.title: Background execution mode (--wait/--background) has no timeout or watchdog — hung processes leak indefinitely
findings.body: commands/review.md adds --wait/--background execution-mode flags and scripts/lib/job-control.mjs was modified (+20 lines). A background review job that hangs (e.g., upstream API never responds, TCP connection stalls) has no visible timeout or watchdog mechanism. The --wait flag presumably polls for completion, but there is no max-wait duration visible in the diff. This means a user running review with --wait could block indefinitely with no feedback, and a --background job could consume resources forever. Under the sampling-sweep context (C3-v04x-cumulative), multiple hung background jobs would accumulate.
findings.recommendation: Add a configurable max-wait timeout (with a sane default, e.g., 10 minutes) to the --wait polling loop. For --background jobs, record a deadline in tracked-jobs state and add a cleanup/reap step that marks timed-out jobs as failed.

findings.title: Missing test coverage for bigmodel-errors retry interaction with actual API error codes
findings.body: scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs is new (168 lines) with a dedicated test file (tests/bigmodel-errors.test.mjs, 189 lines). However, the integration between bigmodel-errors classification and retry.mjs retry-strategy selection is untested at the integration level. If bigmodel-errors classifies a transient error as non-retryable (or vice versa), the retry module will either hammer the API or give up prematurely. The error code mapping in bigmodel-errors.mjs appears to be manually curated against BigModel API docs — any drift between the documented codes and actual API responses will cause silent misclassification.
findings.recommendation: Add an integration test that feeds realistic HTTP error responses (with actual status codes and response bodies from BigModel docs) through the full classify→retry→backoff pipeline and asserts correct retry/no-retry decisions. Consider logging the classification result at debug level for observability.

next_steps: Inspect scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs response handling to confirm non-JSON responses produce structured errors rather than undefined — this is the highest-confidence failure path.
Review scripts/lib/retry.mjs and scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs for crash-safety: trace what happens if SIGTERM arrives between a retry attempt and the tracked-jobs state update.
Check scripts/lib/fs.mjs write paths for atomic-write patterns; if missing, assess whether state corruption on crash is recoverable by the current load logic in state.mjs.
Verify job-control.mjs has a timeout/watchdog for --wait and --background modes before enabling in cumulative sweep runs.
Run the existing test suite (tests/*.test.mjs) against the current tree to confirm no regressions before proceeding with v0.4.7 sweep.
